summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirmed that the DataInput parser bypasses configured depth, document-length, and name-length constraints, and that deep nesting through the DataInput surface reaches OutOfMemoryError while the standard InputStream parser rejects the same payload.
expected_security_impact: The reproduction should show the standard InputStream parser rejecting configured depth, document-length, and name-length violations while the DataInput parser accepts them, and should show a deeply nested DataInput payload triggering OutOfMemoryError under a 256 MB heap.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned commit 22815c439ec1769a60f43c2b971d0cc31f382536, built the reproduction image, confirmed InputStream rejection plus DataInput acceptance for depth, document-length, and name-length checks, confirmed token-count stayed enforced on both paths, and reproduced OutOfMemoryError on ten million nested arrays through the DataInput parser.
verification_method: reproduction/verify.sh compiles the target source tree directly, runs ConstraintBypassHarness and DeepNestingOomHarness, and verifies the exact accepted/rejected/OOM status lines with grep.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
